title: Erucastrum rouyanum
taxonomy: Erucastrum rouyanum is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the order Brassicales. Erucastrum rouyanum is further categorized as a genus within the Brassicaceae family, being closely related to other genera such as Eruca and Arabidopsis. The specific classification of Erucastrum rouyanum is Erucastrum rouyanum (DC.) Coss. & Durieu, indicating its binomial nomenclature. Further classification within the kingdom and phylum is typically as follows, Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Angiosperms, Class: Eudicots, and Order: Brassicales.

intro: Erucastrum rouyanum, also known as Rouyan's rocket, is a perennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, offering potential health benefits in treating conditions such as arthritis, skin infections, and respiratory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Erucastrum rouyanum include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Erucastrum rouyanum is traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its preparations include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
